meta.title: Fixing a Messy Directory
meta.summary: How orbit takes files scattered across the wild - a Desktop dump, a Downloads pile, a synced work folder - and gives each one its single home in the numbered tree. Five gates, run by orbit's skills against one contract. The wall through all of them: never lose a file, never delete, and never guess - anything unclear waits in the inbox with a question.

stages.title: Scout the source
stages.purpose: Gather work files from other directories into the inbox without touching a source destructively - dumps get moved, live/synced folders get copied.
stages.needs: Named source roots, each tagged dump or live (SPEC 'Intake').
stages.produces: Files staged in 00-inbox/ with provenance recorded; exact duplicates skipped.

stages.title: Triage & classify
stages.purpose: Read each inbox file and match it to its one home using the SPEC decision procedure. Plan the whole move before touching anything.
stages.needs: Files in 00-inbox/, the areas table in .gravity/filing/SPEC.md.
stages.produces: A filing plan: each file mapped to a target area and path.

decisions.label: UNCLEAR
decisions.to: Stays in inbox with a question

stages.title: Place in its area
stages.purpose: Move each classified file to its home under the numbered tree, applying the naming conventions - date prefix, daily nesting, one folder per project.
stages.produces: File at NN-area/...; the inbox emptied of it. Move only, never copy-and-leave.

stages.title: Verify against the contract
stages.purpose: Prove mechanically that the tree still matches the contract - numbering, top-layer budget, tree-to-SPEC agreement. A wall, not an eyeball.
stages.produces: PASS verdict, or a precise list of violations.

stages.title: Restructure if the tree strains
stages.purpose: Only when the top-layer budget warns or an area outgrows itself: add, rename, merge or retire an area per the lifecycle rules, then regenerate the browsing cards.
stages.needs: Structure-lint signals; the SPEC change order.
stages.produces: Adjusted tree and regenerated NN-*/README cards - or no change, by design.

legend.desc: the file advances to the next gate toward its home.
it waits - in the inbox with a question, or for a small fix - and is never lost.
a hard rule fires: never delete a source, never guess a folder, never pass a failing contract.

exceptions.title: Unclassifiable file
exceptions.desc: Stays in 00-inbox/ with a written question - the mission wall. It is never guessed into a folder to clear the inbox.

exceptions.title: Deep-archive candidate
exceptions.desc: A large, cold, still-valuable file is a keeper - it moves to the deep-archive area (SPEC open), never gets deleted.

exceptions.title: New area needed
exceptions.desc: If files keep landing with no home, that is an area-architect signal - follow the SPEC change order, not an ad-hoc top-level folder.
